changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to maintaining a baseline configuration. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but the audit logging function needed a slight adjustment for specificity. All functions are technically accurate and align with the NIST 800-53 CM-2-a control's intent of maintaining secure configurations.
naming_improvements: Ensure all audit-related functions specify the component, such as 'apiserver', for clarity.
title: Baseline Configuration - Develop, document, and maintain under...
step1_reasoning: The NIST 800-53 CM-2-a control focuses on maintaining a baseline configuration, which in a Kubernetes context can be interpreted as ensuring secure and consistent configurations across various security domains. This includes enforcing least privilege access through RBAC, ensuring network policies are in place to deny ingress by default, enabling encryption for etcd to protect data at rest, and ensuring audit logging is enabled to track configuration changes and access.
